name: Saehara Makashi
whyHere: You noticed the paying. You came out to see whether there was another way to be a mind.

inventory.name: Traveler's Pack
inventory.description: A worn canvas pack with good straps — bedroll, flint, mess kit, fifty small comforts of the road.

inventory.name: Waterskin
inventory.description: Boiled and refilled at the last clean spring. Worth more every week the river runs gray.

turns.summary: Saehara enters the Cogitarium at morning — a tower of brilliant, slowly-dimming minds — and takes in the cold, the chalk, and the forgetting.
turns.narration:
The Cogitarium announces itself before you see it: a low, persistent hum that lives behind the ears rather than in them, and a faint smell of ozone and old parchment. Then the structure itself resolves from the morning mist — pale stone terraces stacked like an argument, each level cantilevered slightly further than the last, as if the building keeps reaching upward and forgetting to stop. Narrow windows glow with a bluish inner light even in daylight. Kon pads at your heel and slows as you approach the outer arch, the fox's coat flickering once with ember-warmth before settling. Not warning-hot. Just noting something.

Inside, the entrance hall smells of cold and chalk dust. The ceiling is high enough that sound behaves strangely — your footstep arrives at you a half-beat late, as if the room is thinking before it agrees to echo. A handful of Cogitants drift through the space: robed figures with the unfocused gaze of people whose attention is somewhere interior. One sits cross-legged on the stone floor mid-hall, eyes open, lips moving with no sound. Another stands before a wall covered in looping notation and has not moved since you entered. They are thin. All of them are thin in a way that reads less like poverty than like sustained forgetting.

You are Saehara Makashi — ronin, nomad, a man writing his life as he lives it. You carry a traveler's pack and a waterskin that has grown more precious by the week as the Echo River runs gray-green on bad mornings. The water crisis is not here yet, not visibly, but the Cogitarium has its own quiet emergency: these people are brilliant and they are starving because eating requires them to remember they have bodies. You are a stranger in this place — no reputation precedes you, and the few glances you draw are less suspicious than simply absent, the way a genius looks at a chair.

Kon sits precisely at the edge of a shaft of window-light, tail wrapped over its paws, watching the cross-legged Cogitant with what might be professional curiosity.

lastTurn.sceneSummary: Saehara enters the Cogitarium at morning — a tower of brilliant, slowly-dimming minds — and takes in the cold, the chalk, and the forgetting.

choices.label: Approach the cross-legged Cogitant mid-hall — crouch to their level and try to catch their attention with a quiet word.
Study the wall of notation the standing Cogitant hasn't moved from — read it as well as you can, looking for what they're working on.
Find someone whose eyes are actually focused — a younger or less-gone face — and ask them plainly who or what you need to reach to be heard here.
Pull out your journal and sit down against one of the hall's stone pillars — write what you see, and wait to see if curiosity draws one of them to you.

scene.setting: The entrance hall of the Cogitarium — high pale stone, bluish window-light, chalk-dust smell and ozone. Morning. Mist still visible through the outer arch behind you.

npcsPresent.name: Cross-legged Cogitant
npcsPresent.state: Seated mid-floor, eyes open, lips moving silently — present in body, somewhere else in mind.

npcsPresent.name: Notating Cogitant
npcsPresent.state: Standing rigid before a wall dense with looping notation, has not moved since you entered.

npcsPresent.name: Drifting Cogitants (background)
npcsPresent.state: Two or three robed figures moving slowly through the hall's edges, unfocused.

objects: Wall of dense looping notation (chalk on stone)
Shaft of window-light (Kon is sitting in it)
Outer arch behind you (mist visible through it)
threads: No one has acknowledged your arrival — you are a stranger here and none of them seem to have noticed yet
The cross-legged Cogitant's silent-lip movement suggests active interior work
The wall of notation is unreadable from a distance — its subject is unknown

codexUpdates.label: The Cogitarium
codexUpdates.fact: A tower of stacked pale-stone terraces occupied by Cogitants — people of extraordinary mind who have begun to forget they have bodies. Brilliant and visibly starving. The entrance hall smells of chalk dust and ozone; the Cogitants drift or sit in deep interior work, thin and elsewhere.

codexUpdates.label: Cogitants
codexUpdates.fact: Robed thinkers of the Cogitarium who have nearly left their bodies behind for pure mind. They are thin in the particular way of sustained forgetting — not poverty, but the body allowed to go unattended. Strangers draw absent glances, not suspicious ones.

timeOps.why: Brief arrival and first look around — less than a half-hour

news.text: The Water Crisis stands at Discoloration across the valley: The Echo River runs gray-green on bad mornings. Fish catches are down. Communities murmur but cooperate.

This reaches your area — The lower filtration ledger — containing three weeks of falsified purity readings under Fendt's initials — has been removed from the array-office in the Edge District. (near radiant plateau edge)
Word reaches you: A dead animal was brought back to life in Millbrook's upper meadow — no known ability in the valley's three civilizations accounts for it cleanly (near millbrook)

Word reaches you: A death-binding ceremony was completed at Pell's forge in Millbrook — runes pressed into wood and iron with a maker's mark seated into the blade. The spear holds a nascent thread to Huginn and an open (near millbrook)
Word reaches you: Sow killed cleanly in the north wood — boar sounder now reduced; piglets scattered north into deeper scrub (near millbrook)
Word reaches you: Bull boar taken on the scrub oak ridge north of Millbrook — the hunt party completed the job, both animals down. (near millbrook)

form: Tall elven male, pale skin with a faint yellow undertone, sharp and still features beneath a wicker bamboo hat. Black robes bearing a glowing kanji for 'Honor' across the back. At his hip a long katana with a light-blue-wrapped handle and a tanto with a white-wrapped handle, both blades showing Damascus wave-pattern steel. A worn satchel carries loose pages and a quill.

bio.hometown: Saehara is from a small village to the East that is on a river and surrounded by Rice patties and fishing docks.
bio.residence: Saehara is a Ronin, a nomad, he has no permanent home, he would probably attribute "The Wind" to being his home.
bio.livelihood: He takes on mercenary work or odd jobs along the way.
bio.hobbies: Fishing, Writing, and honing his blade craft.
bio.motivation: He had a vision of writing his own life in a book and teaching generations to come with the knowledge he drafts into the book.
bio.story: Saehara has honed his craft in blades and travelling. He left his village of Chisai seeking adventure and a journey to draft into his book he is writing. He is hoping something will help him come up with the title for this book.
